summary: End-to-end testing of the Cloudflare pre-warmed subdomain pool feature (backend + frontend). 13/13 pytest backend tests passed; all UI testids verified; target persistence + clamp behaviour confirmed via reload.
critical_code_review_comments: services/subdomains.py imports get_cloudflare_config from routers.admin and get_platform_settings is imported inside _pool_target — fine, but tight coupling between a service and a router is unusual; consider moving these helpers to a settings/cloudflare service module to avoid circular import risk as the codebase grows.

PlatformSettingsUpdate.subdomain_pool_target uses `int | None` with no Pydantic Field constraints (ge=0, le=50); clamp is applied only on the read path (pool_stats). The raw value (e.g. 999, -5) is therefore stored verbatim in MongoDB. Functionally OK (read-side clamp), but adding `Field(ge=0, le=50)` would reject obviously-bad input at write time with a clean 422 instead of silently saving garbage.

refill_pool / provision_subdomain run sequential `await create_dns_record` calls inside a `for` loop. For target=50 on a slow CF account this is 50 round-trips serial. Consider asyncio.gather with a small semaphore (or at minimum log refill duration) — not blocking now since target≤50 and refill runs in a 3-min scheduler.
seed_data_creation: None. Test creates no persistent fixtures. Side-effect during regression test temporarily set company_name='DeployUnit BV TEST'; restored to 'DeployUnit BV' via cleanup curl after run. subdomain_pool_target restored to its original value via fixture teardown.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Cloudflare is intentionally NOT configured in this preview env. All pool API calls correctly return cloudflare_ready:false and added:0 — NOT a bug. Refill button is disabled in the UI in that state (confirmed). The subdomain_pool_target value is stored raw in platform_settings but clamped to [0,50] on read in services/subdomains._pool_target(); GET /api/admin/subdomain-pool returns the clamped value. Backend test_set_target_invalid_type_returns_422 passes (Pydantic 422 on non-int).
verified_behaviors: GET /api/admin/subdomain-pool: 401 anon, 403 non-admin, 200 admin with full {free,claimed,target,hard_max=50,cloudflare_ready=false,zone_name,upcoming[]}
PUT /api/admin/platform-settings {subdomain_pool_target:20} persisted; GET pool returns target:20
PUT {subdomain_pool_target:999} stored raw; pool target clamped to 50

Frontend Admin → Platform Domain tab: admin-subdomain-pool, pool-free-count ('0 / 10'), pool-health-label ('Cloudflare not configured'), admin-pool-target, admin-pool-refill all visible with correct data-testids
Refill button correctly disabled when cloudflare_ready=false
Changing pool target to 25 → Save → reload → widget shows '0 / 25' and input value persists as 25
Regression: GET /admin/platform-settings (no token leak), PUT without pool target preserves existing pool target, GET /admin/integrations returns full health snapshot
